serviceAccountKey.title: Service Account Key
serviceAccountKey.description: Service account key in JSON format. You can get it from Firebase Console -> Project Settings -> Service accounts -> Generate new private key.

datasetId.title: Dataset ID
datasetId.description: ID of the dataset to import data from.

collection.title: Collection
collection.description: Firestore collection to import data to. If it doesn't exist, it will be created. Note: you can customize the collection for each record using the `transformFunction` input.

documentConflictResolution.title: Document Conflict Resolution
documentConflictResolution.description:
How to handle conflicts when importing data to Firestore:
- *overwrite*: replace existing documents with the same ID.
- *merge*: merge data from the dataset with existing documents.
- *skip*: skip records with existing IDs.

idField.title: ID Field
idField.description: Field in the dataset that will be used as a document ID in Firestore. If not provided, Firestore will generate a random ID for each document. This is useful when you want to update existing documents in Firestore. Note: you can customize the ID for each record using the `transformFunction` input.

transformFunction.title: Transform Function
transformFunction.description: Function that transforms each record from the dataset before importing it to Firestore. The function must return an object (or array of objects) with the `data` key that contains the transformed record and other optional fields. See documentation for more details and examples.
